content_markdown:
A sealed cylinder of length $l$ and cross-sectional area $A$ contains $N$ molecules of an ideal gas at kelvin temperature $T$.

What is the force acting on the area of the cylinder marked $A$ due to the gas?

diagram_metadata.description: A horizontal cylinder with length l indicated by a double-headed arrow below it and cross-sectional area A indicated by a label pointing to the right circular face.

vibe_explanation: The force $F$ on the area $A$ is given by $F = P \times A$. Using the ideal gas law in terms of molecules, $PV = Nk_BT$. Since the volume of the cylinder is $V = A \times l$, we can substitute this into the equation: $P(Al) = Nk_BT$. Rearranging for pressure gives $P = \frac{Nk_BT}{Al}$. Finally, substituting $P$ back into the force equation: $F = \left(\frac{Nk_BT}{Al}\right) \times A = \frac{Nk_BT}{l}$.
